headline: Movers watchlist confirmed 13x vs random; HYP-105/106 long-gapper edges real but magnitude underestimated—shadow test needed before sizing.
what_happened: TICK-036 mining: unfitted 20-name watchlist catches ≥1 of day's top-3 movers on 26.8% of days vs 2.0% random; median top-3 return +90%; serial runners (BNAI 8x) fill ~6% of slots.

HYP-105 confirmed on 71-event holdout (2026-04-08..06-30): Sharpe 3.63, mean +28.5%/event, median +7.9%, win 56.3%, but lookahead leakage flagged—intraday_push/overnight_gap measured through holding window, not usable.

HYP-106 leak-free filter (overnight_gap≤1.24 AND log10(first-min vol)≤5.43) improves HYP-105 skew on n=22: median +7.9%→+67.7%, win 56%→86%, but magnitude assumes unrealistic 09:31 fills on microcaps; real spreads 5-20% + LULD halts unresolved.

HYP-107 honest reconstruction: filter (overnight_gap≤0.577 AND log10 first-min vol≤5.854) on n=57 holdout yields gross median +5.4%, mean +15.3%, win 70%—real signal, ~10x smaller than look-ahead-inflated HYP-106; execution cost unresolved.
Shadow 2026-07-13/14: 3 total signals, constitutional day returns +0.0456% and +0.0042%; 2026-07-15..17: zero signals.
what_it_means: The movers watchlist is a concrete non-random scanner; use it. The long-gapper edges (HYP-105/107) are directionally real and out-of-sample confirmed, but the reported magnitudes are inflated by lookahead leakage and overstated fill assumptions. Gross edge at 09:31 entry on moderate-gap quiet-open runners is ~+5% before slippage—execution costs (1–15% spreads, LULD halts, size limits) will dominate. You need live shadow at 09:31 with realistic halt/spread modeling to know if this is tradeable or academic.
todays_one_thing: Run a forward shadow on HYP-107 filter at 09:31 entry with realistic LULD halt detection and per-tick spread capture; size capacity study on first-minute volume constraint to establish true expected net return and position limits before live sizing.
